examples: Interestingly, the island rule is overturned for small mammals such as rodents, for which gigantism is favored under insular conditions.
Interestingly, the island rule is reversed for small mammals such as rodents, for which gigantism is favored under insular conditions.
There is a phenomenon known as deep-sea gigantism.
Foes of gigantism point out that, so far, it is doing nicely without becoming Manhattan-by-the-Thames.
Yet, alone among the armed services, the Coast Guard suffers from smallness rather than gigantism.
